items.demo:
1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the formal identification and versioning of the document.
   Expected Content: Title, version number, status, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Document and Content Principles Document
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's purpose and significance.
   Expected Content: Summary of the document's objectives, key principles, and intended outcomes.
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the foundational content governance, classification, and lifecycle requirements essential for effective data stewardship within the organization.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance framework.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning based on the maturity score.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Content Management (DCM)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of organizational structure and content generation processes.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being taken within the governance framework.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DCM-01, Define foundational content governance, classification, and lifecycle requirements
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish principles for content governance to enhance data stewardship.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all organizational content categories identified.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objectives.
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and a step-by-step execution structure.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define document and content principles and scope boundaries.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Identification of organizational content categories.
   5.3 Deliverables: Document and content principles document.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Identify Organizational Content Categories
   - Draft Document and Content Principles
   - Align Principles with DAMA Governance Expectations
   - Validate Lifecycle Alignment
   - Document Principles Using DAMA Artefact Structure
   - Review and Approve Principles Document
   - Establish Governance Mechanics
   - Communicate and Train Stakeholders

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To define the structural elements of governance relevant to the action.
   Expected Content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments generating content.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All content types identified in the previous steps.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Authenticity, accessibility, retention, legal value, evidential integrity.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for decision-making.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Stakeholder engagement for content identification
   - Drafting and reviewing principles
   - Lifecycle management and archival processes
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with other governance domains as necessary.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Content types not covered by this governance framework.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the governance of the document itself.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Draft templates for document and content principles.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous governance documents for context.

1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the foundational metadata for the document, ensuring traceability and accountability.
   Expected content: Document title, versioning details, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Data Governance Artifact for DCM-01
   1.2 Version and Status: v1.0 - Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance within the organization.
   Expected content: Summary of the action, its importance, and expected outcomes.
   Sample elements: This document outlines the foundational content governance, classification, and lifecycle requirements necessary for effective data management within the DCM domain.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance action within the organization.
   Expected content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.
   Sample elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Content Management (DCM)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data governance landscape.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly articulate the action being defined, including its objectives and scope.
   Expected content: Action code, title, objectives, and scope boundaries.
   Sample elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DCM-01 - Define foundational content governance, classification, and lifecycle requirements
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish a draft content taxonomy to guide data governance practices.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all content types managed within the DCM domain.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objectives.
   Expected content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and a detailed step-by-step execution structure.
   Sample elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: Draft initial content taxonomy and classification scheme.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires principles defined.
   5.3 Deliverables: Draft content taxonomy v0.1.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Assemble the Team
   - Review Governance Principles
   - Draft Initial Taxonomy
   - Validate Lifecycle Stages
   - Apply Conceptual Modeling Principles
   - Conduct Stewardship Review
   - Refine Taxonomy
   - Obtain Approval

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To define the structural elements that support the governance action.
   Expected content: Coverage areas, governance themes, organizational structure, decision authority, governance processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments involved in content creation and management.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets classified under DCM.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Content governance, classification, lifecycle management.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Roles of Data Owner, Data Steward, and Data Custodian.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Governance Council as the primary decision authority.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Establish governance principles
   - Define content lifecycle stages
   - Review and approve taxonomy
   - Monitor compliance with governance standards
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with Data Quality and Data Security governance frameworks.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes non-DCM related data governance activities.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To define the management and review processes for the document itself.
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.
   Sample elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Office.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the main document.
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample elements:
   9.1 Templates: Draft taxonomy template.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK chapters referenced.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous governance documents related to DCM.

1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the formal control mechanisms for the document.
   Expected Content: Title, versioning, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Data Governance Artifact for DCM-02
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.
   Expected Content: Summary of the action, procedure, and expected outcomes.
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the procedure for assigning stewardship roles for classification, lifecycle, and metadata governance within the DCM domain, ensuring compliance with DAMA standards.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance action.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Classification Management (DCM)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Medium-sized enterprise with a focus on data governance
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 1 - Initial

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being taken within the governance framework.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DCM-02 - Assign stewardship roles for classification, lifecycle, and metadata governance
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish clear stewardship roles to enhance data governance practices.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all data assets classified under DCM.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the step-by-step execution structure for the governance procedure.
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To consolidate document and content requirements effectively.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Completion of DCM-01
   5.3 Deliverables: Content requirement registers
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Gather Existing Documentation
   - Consolidate Requirements
   - Validate Metadata Requirements
   - Ensure Semantic Clarity
   - Confirm Stewardship and Compliance Roles
   - Prioritize High-Risk Content Classes
   - Review and Validate Requirements
   - Document and Distribute Final Register

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To define the structural components of governance relevant to the action.
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments handling data classified under DCM
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets requiring classification and lifecycle management
   6.3 Governance Themes: Classification, lifecycle management, metadata governance
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Owner accountable for data domain decisions
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Document collection and review
   - Metadata validation
   - Role assignment
   - Risk prioritization
   - Stakeholder review sessions
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Security governance
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Non-DCM related data assets

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the governance of the document itself.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Office
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Content requirement register template
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK references
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous governance documents related to DCM

1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the foundational metadata for the document, ensuring traceability and accountability.
   Expected Content:
   1.1 Document Title
   - Title of the document (e.g., Content Standards Manual)
   1.2 Version and Status
   - Current version number and status (e.g., Draft, Final)
   1.3 Approval Authority
   - Name of the approving body (e.g., Data Governance Council)
   1.4 Revision History
   - Log of changes made to the document, including dates and descriptions of revisions.

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's purpose and significance.
   Expected Content:
   - Brief description of the Content Standards Manual and its role in data governance.
   - Summary of key objectives and expected outcomes.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance framework.
   Expected Content:
   3.1 Domain Definition
   - Definition of the Data Classification and Management (DCM) domain.
   3.2 Organizational Context
   - Overview of the organization’s data landscape and governance environment.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning (based on Score)
   - Statement of the current maturity score (Score 2) and implications for governance practices.

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clarify the specific action being addressed within the governance framework.
   Expected Content:
   4.1 Action Code and Title
   - Action Code: DCM-03
   - Action Title: Approve classification, retention, metadata, and repository standards
   4.2 Action Objective
   - Objective of the action in relation to data governance.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries
   - Definition of the scope of the action, including limitations and exclusions.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.
   Expected Content:
   5.1 Procedure Objective
   - Objective of the procedure in defining document and content standards.
   5.2 Prerequisites
   - Requirement of v1.0 framework for execution.
   5.3 Deliverables
   - Identification of the Content Standards Manual as the primary deliverable.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure
   - Detailed steps for execution:
     - Define Naming and Versioning Standards
     - Establish Metadata Standards
     - Develop Classification and Retention Policies
     - Validate Semantic Alignment
     - Map Document Lifecycle Stages
     - Engage Governance Roles for Stewardship Acceptance
     - Finalize and Compile the Content Standards Manual
     - Communicate and Train Stakeholders
     - Establish Review and Update Mechanism

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To delineate the governance framework and its components.
   Expected Content:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage
   - Description of the organizational units involved in governance.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage
   - Identification of data assets governed by the standards.
   6.3 Governance Themes
   - Key themes guiding the governance approach (e.g., compliance, accountability).
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure
   - Diagram or description of the governance structure, including roles.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model
   - Explanation of decision-making authority within the governance framework.
   6.6 Governance Processes (bullet structured)
   - Document Creation Process
   - Document Review Process
   - Document Approval Process
   - Document Retention Process
   - Document Disposal Process
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures
   - Description of how this governance framework interacts with other governance frameworks.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions
   - Identification of areas or data types excluded from this governance framework.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   7.1 Monitoring Structure
       - Define documented monitoring approach without performance targets
       - Assign responsible governance roles
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment
       - Define alignment with internal control and policy frameworks
   7.3 Escalation Model
       - Define formal escalation pathways and decision authority levels
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the governance of the document itself.
   Expected Content:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management
   - Description of the lifecycle stages of the document.
   8.2 Review Cycle
   - Schedule for regular reviews of the document.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document
   - Identification of the Data Owner responsible for the document.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.
   Expected Content:
   9.1 Templates
   - Sample templates for documentation and standards.
   9.2 Reference Standards
   - List of standards referenced in the document (e.g., DAMA-DMBOK).
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts
   - Additional documents or resources that support the governance framework.

1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the formal identification and versioning of the document.
   Expected Content: Title, version number, status, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Data Governance Artifact for DCM-04
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.
   Expected Content: Summary of the action, procedure, and expected outcomes.
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the procedures for embedding lifecycle rules, ethical safeguards, and repository routines into daily operations to ensure content governance adherence.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance action.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Content Management (DCM)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Medium-sized enterprise with a focus on data compliance
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 3 - Managed

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being taken within the governance framework.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DCM-04 - Embed lifecycle rules, ethical safeguards, and repository routines into routines
   4.2 Action Objective: To ensure consistent and ethical data management throughout its lifecycle.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applies to all data assets managed under the DCM domain.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the step-by-step execution structure for the governance procedure.
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: Track content governance adherence effectively.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires monitoring routines to be established.
   5.3 Deliverables: Content compliance register.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Define Governance Metrics
   - Establish Monitoring Routines
   - Validate Lifecycle Transitions
   - Confirm Metadata Accuracy
   - Record Findings as Governed Artifacts
   - Prioritize Remediation Actions
   - Review and Approve Remediation Actions
   - Implement Remediation Actions
   - Update Content Compliance Register

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To delineate the organizational and procedural structures supporting governance.
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, governance processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments handling data assets.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data types managed under DCM.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Compliance, quality, lifecycle management.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Stewards.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Owner accountable for data domain decisions.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Establish governance metrics
   - Implement monitoring routines
   - Conduct lifecycle audits
   - Validate metadata accuracy
   - Document compliance findings
   - Prioritize remediation actions
   - Review remediation plans
   - Execute remediation actions
   - Update compliance registers
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Security governance.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes non-DCM related data assets.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs
       - Define measurable indicators aligned with the action objective
       - Define measurement frequency and reporting cadence
       - Identify data source for each KPI
       - Assign accountable monitoring role
   7.2 Compliance and Internal Control Alignment
       - Reference structured internal control mechanisms
       - Define validation checkpoints
   7.3 Operational Escalation Model
       - Define escalation thresholds
       - Assign responsible escalation authority
       - Define documented resolution workflow
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the document itself.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Scheduled reviews every six months.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Owned by the Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Governance metrics framework template.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Examples of governed artifacts register.
10. Evidence Required
- Approved document with version control
- Formal approval record
- RACI matrix attached
- Repository registration ID
- Stakeholder validation evidence

11. Acceptance Criteria
- Document formally approved
- Scope clearly defined
- Roles validated
- Deliverables stored in official repository
- Communication recorded

12. Control Owner
- Primary Owner: Domain Owner or Data Governance Office
- Oversight Authority: Data Governance Council
- Escalation Path: Chief Data Officer

1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the formal control and management of the document.
   Expected Content: Title, versioning, approval details, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Automated Content Rule Set
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.
   Expected Content: Summary of the action, its importance, and expected outcomes.
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the automated content validation rules necessary for ensuring data quality and compliance within the Data Governance framework.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance action.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Classification Management (DCM)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Medium-sized enterprise with a focus on data-driven decision-making.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 4 - Controlled

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being taken within the governance framework.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DCM-06 - Automate classification, lifecycle, metadata, and quality validation
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish a robust automated content validation framework.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applies to all data assets within the DCM domain.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the step-by-step execution of the governance procedure.
   Expected Content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define and implement automated content validation rules.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires validation criteria established by data stewards.
   5.3 Deliverables: Comprehensive automated content rule set.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Define validation criteria.
   - Capture rules in metadata.
   - Validate lifecycle appropriateness.
   - Conduct CMMI automation readiness checks.
   - Document the automated content rule set.
   - Assign roles and governance structure.

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To delineate the governance framework supporting the action.
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All data management functions within the organization.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets classified under DCM.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, compliance, and lifecycle management.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Governance Council holds primary decision authority.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Establish validation criteria.
   - Document rules and procedures.
   - Monitor compliance with automated rules.
   - Review and update governance documentation.
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Architecture governance frameworks.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes non-DCM related data assets.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   7.1 Performance Monitoring Framework
       - Define KPI formula and calculation method
       - Define target values and tolerance thresholds
       - Define alert and breach triggers
       - Define corrective action activation criteria
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Verification
       - Define audit sampling methodology
       - Define validation frequency
       - Define evidence retention requirements
       - Align with Internal Audit Function oversight
   7.3 Controlled Escalation Model
       - Define formal SLA timelines for issue resolution
       - Define mandatory reporting to Data Governance Council
       - Define accountability tracking mechanism
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the document.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycles, and ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually or upon significant changes.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Scheduled reviews every six months.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office is responsible for document maintenance.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Automated content rule set template.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous validation criteria documents and reports.
10. Evidence Required
- Approved document with version control
- Formal approval record
- RACI matrix attached
- Repository registration ID
- Stakeholder validation evidence

11. Acceptance Criteria
- Document formally approved
- Scope clearly defined
- Roles validated
- Deliverables stored in official repository
- Communication recorded

12. Control Owner
- Primary Owner: Domain Owner or Data Governance Office
- Oversight Authority: Data Governance Council
- Escalation Path: Chief Data Officer

13. Performance Metrics
- % of domains with approved artifacts
- % of reviews within SLA
- % of procedures with valid evidence
- Average update cycle time
- Non-compliance incidents per cycle

14. Monitoring Dashboard
- Domain compliance status
- Pending review alerts
- Governance heatmap
- KPI performance summary

15. Compliance Validation Cycle
- Mandatory semiannual review
- Annual internal audit
- Cross-domain validation
- RACI revalidation
- Escalation protocol

1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the formal control mechanisms for the document.
   Expected Content: Title, versioning, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Data Governance Artifact for DCM-09
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Approved
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Initial release on [Date], updates on [Date].

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.
   Expected Content: Summary of the action, procedure, and expected outcomes.
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the procedure for continuously refining content governance and lifecycle using evidence, aimed at enhancing content standards based on insights derived from trend analysis.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance action.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Content Management (DCM)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Medium-sized enterprise with a focus on data-driven decision-making.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 5 - Optimized.

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being addressed.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DCM-09 - Continuously refine content governance and lifecycle using evidence
   4.2 Action Objective: To enhance content governance frameworks through evidence-based insights.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applies to all content governance standards within the organization.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To detail the procedural steps necessary for executing the action.
   Expected Content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and a step-by-step execution structure.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: Improve content standards based on insights.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires trend analysis.
   5.3 Deliverables: Updated content standards.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Conduct Trend Analysis
   - Identify Improvement Areas
   - Validate Against DAMA Metadata Definitions
   - Align with Lifecycle Expectations
   - Revalidate Stewardship Roles
   - Draft Updated Content Standards
   - Review and Approve Changes
   - Communicate Changes
   - Implement and Monitor

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To outline the governance framework supporting the action.
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments involved in content management.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets related to content governance.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Continuous improvement, compliance, and alignment with organizational goals.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council overseeing the Data Governance Office.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Owner responsible for content governance decisions.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Trend analysis execution
   - Stakeholder engagement for feedback
   - Documentation of changes
   - Approval workflow for updated standards
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Architecture governance.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes non-content related data governance activities.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   7.1 Automated Performance Monitoring
       - Define automated KPI data capture mechanisms
       - Define automated threshold enforcement
       - Define automated alerting and exception logging
   7.2 Continuous Compliance and Adaptive Governance
       - Define multi-cycle trend analysis requirements
       - Define root cause clustering and preventive redesign triggers
       - Embed audit integration within governance operating model
   7.3 Strategic Escalation Optimization
       - Define data-driven escalation prioritization
       - Define decision optimization mechanisms
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization
       7.4.1 Quantitative multi-cycle performance improvement evidence
       7.4.2 Before vs After KPI comparative metrics
       7.4.3 Governance automation proof points
       7.4.4 External benchmark alignment reference
       7.4.5 Documented governance operating model integration
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the governance of the document itself.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Scheduled reviews every six months.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Owned by the Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Standard template for content standards documentation.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous trend analysis reports and validation documents.
10. Evidence Required
- Approved document with version control
- Formal approval record
- RACI matrix attached
- Repository registration ID
- Stakeholder validation evidence
